report_name: Oracle Announces July 2026 Critical Patch Update with 1,455 Fixes

vulnerabilities.name: Critical Oracle Analytics Vulnerability July 2026
vulnerabilities.description: A critical vulnerability in Oracle Analytics products with a CVSS v3.1 base score of 9.9. The vulnerability is remotely exploitable without authentication and could allow an attacker to take full control of the affected system.

vulnerabilities.name: Oracle Autonomous Health Framework Vulnerability July 2026
vulnerabilities.description: A vulnerability in Oracle Autonomous Health Framework with a CVSS score of 8.1 that is remotely exploitable without authentication.

vulnerabilities.name: Unspecified Oracle Database Vulnerabilities July 2026
vulnerabilities.description: Multiple unspecified critical vulnerabilities in Oracle Database products, including Oracle Database 19c and Oracle AI Database 26ai, addressed in the July 2026 Critical Patch Update.

indicators.name: Anomalous Oracle BI Publisher Access
indicators.pattern:
title: Anomalous Oracle BI Publisher Access
id: 2b8a1c3d-f4e5-4b9a-8c7d-6e5f4a3b2c1d
status: experimental
description: Monitors for access to the default Oracle BI Publisher URL path (/xmlpserver). Anomalous requests to this path could indicate attempts to exploit vulnerabilities in Oracle Analytics.
references:
  - https://www.oracle.com/security-alerts/cpujul2026.html
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-07-20
logsource:
  category: proxy
detection:
  selection:
    c-uri-stem|contains: '/xmlpserver'
  condition: selection
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate administrative access to Oracle BI Publisher.
level: medium
indicators.description: This indicator detects web requests to the default URL path for Oracle BI Publisher. The article suggests monitoring this path for anomalous activity that could indicate attempts to exploit vulnerabilities in Oracle Analytics.

indicators.name: Suspicious Oracle Database Connection Attempts
indicators.pattern:
title: Suspicious Oracle Database Connection Attempts
id: 9d8c7b6a-5f4e-4d3c-2b1a-0e9f8d7c6b5a
status: experimental
description: Monitors for network connections to the default Oracle Database listener port (1521). While this is normal traffic, a high volume of unusual or unauthorized connection attempts from unexpected sources could indicate scanning or exploitation attempts.
references:
  - https://www.oracle.com/security-alerts/cpujul2026.html
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-07-20
logsource:
  category: network_connection
detection:
  selection:
    DestinationPort: 1521
  condition: selection
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate client connections to the Oracle database.
level: informational
indicators.description: This indicator detects network traffic to the default Oracle Database listener port (1521). The article suggests monitoring this port for unusual or unauthorized connection attempts.

indicators.name: Suspicious Child Process of Oracle Database Process
indicators.pattern:
title: Suspicious Child Process of Oracle Database Process
id: 5a4b3c2d-1e0f-4a9b-8c7d-6e5f4a3b2c1d
status: experimental
description: Detects child processes spawned by the main Oracle database process (oracle.exe on Windows, oracle on Linux). This could indicate post-exploitation activity such as remote code execution following a successful compromise.
references:
  - https://www.oracle.com/security-alerts/cpujul2026.html
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-07-20
logsource:
  product: windows
  category: process_creation
detection:
  selection:
    ParentImage|endswith: '\oracle.exe'
  filter_known_good:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\cmd.exe' # Example, should be tuned for environment
      - '\powershell.exe' # Example, should be tuned for environment
  condition: selection and not filter_known_good
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate administrative scripts or tools spawned by the database process.
level: high
indicators.description: This indicator detects potentially malicious child processes spawned by the main Oracle database process. The article suggests looking for unexpected child processes as a sign of exploitation.

relationships.source_name: Suspicious Child Process of Oracle Database Process
